mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals an impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad demand contraction, leading to negative equity returns as corporate profit margins compress and inventory buildup forces production cuts.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, accelerating industrial production reflects capacity utilization rebound and supply chain normalization, driving positive forward equity returns over a quarter as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate corporate revenue base, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings momentum translates directly into price appreciation.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding a 2.5 percent annualized growth rate forces tighter monetary policy expectations, raising discount rates and equity risk premiums, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes do not lead to negative equity returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job creation combined with sub-5.5 percent unemployment signals a tight labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a quarter as earnings stability improves.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k while UNRATE falls below 5.5%
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, a peak and subsequent decline in industrial production signals supply-side saturation and impending demand weakness, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not lead to negative equity returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a rolling 3-year window.
